coverage_matrix.best_real_source: vLLM rate(vllm:request_success_total[1m]), Ray Serve rate(ray_serve_num_ongoing_requests_total[1m]), Triton nv_inference_count rate
Kubernetes K8sPlacementSnapshot.running_gpu_pods or Ray Serve ray_serve_deployment_replica_count
vLLM vllm:request_first_token_seconds (histogram p99) or Ray Serve ray_serve_request_latency_ms (p99)
coverage_matrix.caveat: queue percentile is end-to-end latency p99, not pure queue wait; vLLM has TTFT histogram; Triton averages only

coverage_matrix.best_real_source: vLLM e2e_p50 OR Triton nv_inference_queue_duration_us / exec_count (average)
vLLM e2e p99, Ray Serve request_latency p99
coverage_matrix.caveat: Triton default metrics expose averages only; histograms must be enabled in Triton config

coverage_matrix.best_real_source: resolve_timeout_pct() fallback chain: A=explicit timeout counter, B=deadline_exceeded counter, C=error_rate_pct, D=latency_p99 > SLA proxy
coverage_matrix.caveat: Levels A/B are not exposed by mainstream serving runtimes today; the bridge auto-selects the best available level and records it on the FieldProvenance.fallback_level field

coverage_matrix.best_real_source: derive_sla_violation_pct(latency_p99_ms, latency_sla_p99_ms) - per-tick binary, aggregated over a window for a meaningful share
coverage_matrix.caveat: DERIVED signal; binary per-tick (100.0 when latency_p99 > sla, else 0.0); only meaningful aggregated over a window

coverage_matrix.best_real_source: compute_k8s_scale_delta(prev_snapshot, curr_snapshot, owner_name=...) - deterministic pod-set delta across two K8sPlacementSnapshots
coverage_matrix.caveat: Requires two snapshots; uses (namespace, pod_name) as identity key so rolling restarts are visible as churn

coverage_matrix.best_real_source: compute_k8s_scale_delta(...).churn_count - distinct from replica_delta so rolling restarts (churn>0, replica_delta=0) are separable from scale-ups

coverage_matrix.caveat: vLLM-only; Triton / Ray Serve do not surface this

coverage_matrix.best_real_source: aurelius/residency/ingest.py adapt_vllm + pilot per-request CSV per PILOT_TELEMETRY_CONTRACT.md
coverage_matrix.caveat: vLLM cannot emit per-request load timestamps; pilot must instrument the model-loader hook

queue_p99_ms.note: no public trace ships realized queue percentiles per tick

timeout_pct.note: no public trace ships a real timeout rate per tick

active_replicas.real_source: K8s pod count + Ray Serve replica count

prefix_cache_hit_rate.note: not present in any committed public trace; vLLM-only signal

model_residency / cold_start.note: GenAI 2026 request<->infra layers are no_join; MIT has node-level GPU residency only

can_calibration_run_on_real_telemetry_to.triton_pilot: PARTIAL - p95/p99 latency requires Triton histograms enabled

remaining_gaps_before_pilot_production_c: Levels A/B of timeout fallback (explicit per-second timeout / deadline_exceeded counters) are not exposed by mainstream serving runtimes today; bridge uses C_ERROR_RATE or D_SLA_RISK_PROXY

SGLang Prometheus adapter remains BLOCKED_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN - no fixture, no registry; skipped test documents the gap (see tests/test_pilot_telemetry_wiring.py::test_sglang_adapter_blocked_schema_unknown)
Per-request cold-start timestamps require pilot model-loader instrumentation (vLLM cannot self-report)
Real-cluster execution remains disabled by default - calibration produces shadow-mode recommendations only

wiring_improvements_2026_05_31.telemetry_provenance_module: aurelius/frontier/telemetry_provenance.py: FieldOrigin enum (REAL/DERIVED/PROXY/SIMULATED/MISSING), FieldProvenance, TickProvenance, TimeoutFallback enum (A->D), resolve_timeout_pct(), derive_sla_violation_pct()
wiring_improvements_2026_05_31.bridge_with_provenance: aurelius/frontier/dynamic_telemetry.py::telemetry_tick_with_provenance_from_inference_service_state - returns (tick, TickProvenance) so callers can audit per-field provenance
wiring_improvements_2026_05_31.k8s_scale_delta: aurelius/connectors/kubernetes.py::compute_k8s_scale_delta(prev, curr, owner_name, namespace) -> K8sReplicaDelta (replicas, scale_events, churn_count, added/removed pod names)
wiring_improvements_2026_05_31.vllm_preemptions: aurelius/connectors/vllm.py now scrapes vllm:num_preemptions_total into InferenceServiceState.preemptions_total; registered as inference.preemptions_per_second in vllm_registry()
wiring_improvements_2026_05_31.calibration_summary_provenance_rollup: compute_frontier_calibration_summary() now emits a telemetry_provenance block counting origins per field, plus timeout_fallback_counts, when records carry tick_provenance
